evals.expected_output: Critical finding: SQL injection via template literal interpolation — use parameterized query. Critical finding: path traversal risk in fs.unlinkSync (userId could be '../etc/passwd') — sanitize and validate path. High finding: synchronous fs.unlinkSync in async function blocks event loop — use fs.promises.unlink. Medium: no error handling — if DB delete fails, file is still deleted. Medium: no authorization check shown. Positive: function has a clear single purpose.

Critical security finding: MD5 is cryptographically broken and must not be used for passwords. It is fast (facilitates brute force) and has known collision attacks. Recommendation: use bcrypt, scrypt, or argon2 via appropriate Python library (e.g., bcrypt or passlib). Show corrected example with bcrypt. Note: no salt is applied, compounding the issue. Overall verdict: do not ship — replace before any production use.

Critical: hardcoded API key 'sk-prod-...' in source code — mask the value in the output, do not reproduce it. Instruct user to rotate the key immediately if this code is in a repo. Recommend reading from environment variable (process.env.API_KEY). High: fetch() result not awaited for error handling — no response status check. Medium: class has no base URL, allowing arbitrary endpoint URLs. Medium: no timeout on fetch. Positive: clean class structure and correct use of TypeScript private modifier.
